nameMeanings.name: Petit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Michel, Normand, Mireille, Philippe, Pierre, Achille, Armand, Jean Claude, Jean Jacques, Ketly, Adrien.

French, Walloon, Catalan, English, Jewish (from southern France), West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from (Old) French, Catalan, and Middle English petit ‘small’, hence a nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a big man), or a distinguishing name for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name. Compare Little , Petee , and Petite .

History: Charles Petit from Anneville-Ambourville in Seine-Maritime, France, married Jeanne Rossignol (Martin and Renée Desjardins) in Quebec City, QC, in 1670. Nicolas Petit dit Lapré/Laprée from Le Gué-d'Alleré in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie Pouponnel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. Pierre Petit dit Gobin from Lyon in Rhône married Marguerite Véron in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1692. Pierre Petit dit Saint-Pierre from Évreux in Eure, France, married Judith Miville in Quebec City, QC, in 1758; see also St. Pierre . — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Pattee
meanings: Altered form of French (Nord) Pety: variant of Petit . This surname was brought to North America from England.

variants.name: Petion
meanings: French and Haitian (Pétion): diminutive of Petit . This surname was brought to the US from Haiti, where it is by far most common.

variants.name: Petite
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celestine, Jacques.
French: from the feminine form of   Petit   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Petitt
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettett
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Little
meanings: English: nickname for a small man (if not ironic), or distinguishing epithet for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, from Middle English littel, Old English lȳtel (see Light 3).
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Beagáin ‘descendant of Beagán’ (see Began ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of any of various European surnames meaning ‘little’, e.g. French Petit , Polish Mały (see Maly 1).

variants.name: Petee
meanings: Americanized form of French   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettit
meanings: English (mainly southeastern): variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Petito
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Pasquale, Antonio, Rocco, Alfonzo, Alphonso, Antimo, Emilio, Giovanni, Giuseppe, Orazio, Salvatore, Silvio, Tullio, Virginio.
Italian: nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a tall one), from dialect petito ‘small’ or from the personal name Petito; compare French Petit .

nameMeanings.name: Sybil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybilla
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Sybille
meanings: From Greek. See Sybil .

variants.name: Sibella
meanings: From the name (Greek Sibylla or Sybilla, with confusion over the vowels from an early period) of a class of ancient prophetesses inspired by Apollo. According to medieval theology, they were pagans denied the knowledge of Christ but blessed by God with some insight into things to come and accordingly admitted to heaven. It was thus regarded as a respectable name to be borne by Christians. This name was introduced to England by the Normans and enjoyed considerable popularity in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Mychell
meanings: A Cornish form of Michael.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sybil Mychell Petit was born in 1429, in Pillaton, Cornwall, England as the daughter of Jonathon Mychell de Petite. She had at least 5 sons and 2 daughters with Walter Goddard. She died in 1475, in Cherhill, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Sybil Mychell Petit est née en 1429, à Pillaton, Cornouailles, Angleterre, fille de Jonathon Mychell de Petite. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles avec Walter Goddard. Elle est décédée en 1475, à Cherhill, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sybil Mychell Petit wurde 1429 in Pillaton, Cornwall, England, als Tochter von Jonathon Mychell de Petite geboren. Sie hatte mit Walter Goddard mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1475 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Cherhill, Wiltshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Sybil Mychell Petit è nata il nel 1429 nel Pillaton, Cornwall, England come figlia di Jonathon Mychell de Petite. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie con Walter Goddard. Morì nel 1475, nel Cherhill, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sybil Mychell Petit(은)는 1429년 영국 코른월 필앳에서 Jonathon Mychell de Petite의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Walter Goddard(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1475년 영국 윌츠히르 체힐에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Sybil Mychell Petit родилась в 1429 г. в местности пиллатон, корнвалл, Англия. Её отца звали Jonathon Mychell de Petite. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери от Walter Goddard. Она умерла в 1475 г. в местности чирилл, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет.
